name: Benben Stone
description: The Benben Stone is a sacred stone in ancient Egyptian mythology, representing the primordial mound that arose from the waters of Nun at the beginning of creation. It is believed to be the place where the god Atum first stood and initiated the act of creation. The Benben Stone is often depicted as a pyramidal or conical stone, and it is associated with the sun god Ra. It is considered a symbol of rebirth, regeneration, and the sun's daily journey across the sky. The Benben Stone served as the prototype for the capstones of pyramids and obelisks, connecting earthly structures with the divine realm. Its presence on these structures was meant to imbue them with the power of creation and the eternal nature of the gods. The stone is not just a physical object but a potent symbol of the cosmos, the origin of life, and the enduring power of the sun. Its connection to Atum and Ra solidifies its importance as a central element in the Egyptian understanding of the universe and their place within it. The Benben Stone, therefore, embodies the very essence of creation and the cyclical nature of existence as understood by the ancient Egyptians. It is more than a stone; it is a representation of the dawn of time and the continuous renewal of the world.
subtitle: Primordial mound of creation
material: Unknown, possibly meteoric iron or a type of igneous rock
origin: Emerged from the primordial waters of Nun
currentLocation: Unknown, possibly lost or destroyed, representations exist in various museums

keyMyths.title: Creation Myth
keyMyths.description: The Benben Stone is the first land to appear from the waters of Nun. Atum stands on it to begin creation, either through masturbation or through uttering the names of things to bring them into existence.

keyMyths.title: Pyramidion Symbolism
keyMyths.description: The pyramidion, the capstone of a pyramid, is believed to be a representation of the Benben Stone. This symbolizes the pyramid's connection to the sun and the act of creation, ensuring the pharaoh's rebirth.

sources.author: R.T. Rundle Clark
sources.work: Myth and Symbol in Ancient Egypt
sources.description: Explores the symbolism and mythology of ancient Egyptian culture.
sources.text: Explores the symbolism and mythology of ancient Egyptian culture.
